repo_prior_art.cite: No completed in-catalogue result supplies this repaired latent-response formulation; failed pid_mediation_proxy_v1 used invalid bridge-fibre geometry.

published_prior_art.cite: DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 gives sharp polynomial-program representations for declared discrete SCMs.

repo_prior_art.cite: No completed catalogue entry gives this exact endpoint-face formulation.

published_prior_art.cite: It is a compact-feasibility reformulation of the no-proxy endpoint comparison, not yet a distinct strict-refinement result.

repo_prior_art.cite: No active catalogue entry states this response-type reduction.

published_prior_art.cite: Sjolander2009 and BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 provide the randomized-treatment natural-effect no-proxy bounds.

repo_prior_art.cite: Failed pid_mediation_proxy_v1 already pursued observable proxy endpoint exclusion; this core's finite latent-realizable factorization is a material repair, not a completed duplicate.

published_prior_art.cite: No verified source gives necessary-and-sufficient observed signed-minor/rank conditions and a positive binary witness for strict factorization-based contraction of the NDE no-proxy interval.

structure_flags.label: Cluster routing
structure_flags.one_line: This is a potential-outcomes response-function partial-identification problem, not a graph-native SCM result stated over a DAG/ADMG or do-calculus query; route it to PartialID or add the required graphical SCM formulation.

novelty_flags.label: Open-ended Question 1 / related work
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey misses closer mediation-bound comparators, especially HanLuoZhangGu2025 on partially observed mediator-endpoint confounders and LuoGeng2016 on direct/indirect-effect bounds; state precisely why proxy-factorized endpoint exclusion is not covered.

soundness_flags.label: def:binary-positive-law-class
soundness_flags.one_line: P₂⁺ uses treatment-conditional cells p_azwmy but does not require 0<P(A=a)<1, so those conditionals and F₂(P) are undefined for laws with a zero-probability treatment arm.

soundness_flags.label: Open-ended Question 1 / honest scope
soundness_flags.one_line: The advertised failure-of-completeness regime is never defined or imposed: a full-rank/completeness-satisfying binary proxy law can meet P₂⁺ and satisfy the requested witness, so it would not answer the stated question.

soundness_flags.label: def:binary-certificate-handle
soundness_flags.one_line: A nonconvex polynomial feasibility problem has no defined rational primal-dual program here; replace that phrase with a specified quantifier-elimination or Positivstellensatz-certificate route.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 1
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The certificate and any strict contraction are conditional on the at-most-two-class, conditionally independent proxy-factorization model; no unrestricted-latent-cardinality or inference claim is made.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Observable endpoint exclusion only UNDER the stated finite latent-class proxy-factorization model.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Define the finite completeness operator and explicitly restrict the certificate/witness to its failure regime; add treatment-arm positivity to P₂⁺.
Route the kernel to PartialID, or specify a DAG/ADMG and a graph-native compatible-SCM class that exactly induces the response-function factorization.

Use quantifier elimination to obtain either a concrete positive rational strict-contraction witness with verified completeness failure or a theorem that no such witness exists; only then seek a compact signed-minor/rank characterization.
Add and distinguish HanLuoZhangGu2025 and LuoGeng2016, alongside the existing Breum, Ghassami, and Dukes comparisons.

recommended_next_step: Revise the model domain and completeness-failure target, replace the undefined primal-dual handle, add the missed mediation-bound comparisons, then rerun novelty review.
